# Fil One Documentation

> Fil One is S3-compatible object storage built for AI agents and data-intensive
> workloads. Backed by the Filecoin network for cryptographically verifiable data
> integrity. No egress fees, no per-request charges, $4.99/TB/month flat rate.
> Use any S3-compatible SDK or tool — point it at `https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one`
> with region `eu-west-1` and standard Access Key ID / Secret Access Key auth.

Critical integration notes for developers:
- Buckets must be created via the dashboard (app.fil.one) — CreateBucket and
  DeleteBucket via S3 API are not supported and return AccessDenied
- Path-style addressing required: `https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one/bucket/key`
  (virtual-hosted-style URLs are not supported)
- Authentication: AWS Signature Version 4 (SigV4) — all S3-compatible SDKs
  handle this automatically
- All buckets are private by default; all data is encrypted at rest (AES-256
  SSE-KMS) and in transit (TLS); encryption cannot be disabled
- Single region currently: eu-west-1 (additional regions planned)
- Deletion is permanent and immediate — no recycle bin or soft-delete

## Getting Started

- [Quickstart](https://docs.fil.one/quickstart): Create an account, generate
  API keys, and upload your first object — covers App, AWS CLI, Python, and
  JavaScript paths
- [Quickstart - AWS CLI](https://docs.fil.one/quickstart/cli): Full CLI walkthrough
  including named profile setup to avoid repeating --endpoint-url
- [Quickstart - SDK](https://docs.fil.one/quickstart/sdk): Copy-paste examples
  for Python (boto3), JavaScript (AWS SDK v3), and Go to create a bucket, upload,
  and verify

## Storage

- [Buckets](https://docs.fil.one/storage/buckets): Bucket naming rules, how to
  create via dashboard, listing via API, and Filecoin deal lifecycle on deletion
- [Objects](https://docs.fil.one/storage/objects): Upload, download, list, delete,
  and presigned URL generation with code examples for App, AWS CLI, and boto3
- [Multipart Uploads](https://docs.fil.one/storage/multipart-uploads): Required
  for objects > 5 GB; recommended for > 100 MB. Includes AWS CLI and boto3
  TransferConfig examples, part limits, and cleanup of incomplete uploads

## Security

- [Authentication](https://docs.fil.one/security/authentication): SigV4 auth
  setup for AWS CLI, Python (boto3), JavaScript (AWS SDK v3), and Go; credential
  best practices including environment variable usage
- [API Keys](https://docs.fil.one/security/api-keys): Creating keys with scoped
  permissions (Read/Write/List/Delete), bucket-level scoping, key expiration,
  revocation, and security best practices
- [Encryption](https://docs.fil.one/security/encryption): AES-256 SSE-KMS
  encryption always on by default for all objects; TLS in transit; optional
  client-side encryption supported

## Billing

- [Trial & Billing](https://docs.fil.one/billing/trial): 30-day free trial
  includes 1 TB storage and 2 TB egress; trial lifecycle (active → grace period
  → expiry); paid plan at $4.99/TB/month with no egress or request fees
- [Pricing](https://docs.fil.one/billing/pricing): $4.99/TB/month, no egress
  fees, no per-request charges, no hidden costs

## API Reference

- [API Reference Overview](https://docs.fil.one/reference/overview): Base endpoint,
  SigV4 authentication requirement, path-style addressing, and response format
- [S3 Compatibility](https://docs.fil.one/reference/s3-compatibility): Full table
  of supported vs. unsupported S3 operations (GetObject, PutObject, DeleteObject,
  ListObjectsV2, multipart, presigned URLs all supported; bucket policies, lifecycle
  rules, IAM not yet supported); differences from AWS S3
- [Error Reference](https://docs.fil.one/reference/errors): Standard S3 XML error
  format, full table of 4xx and 5xx error codes, and troubleshooting for the most
  common errors (SignatureDoesNotMatch, ServiceUnavailable)

## SDKs & Tools

- [SDKs & Tools Overview](https://docs.fil.one/sdk/overview): Connection parameters
  for all tools (endpoint, region, credentials); recommended tools list including
  rclone, Cyberduck, s3cmd, MinIO Client
- [Python (boto3)](https://docs.fil.one/sdk/python): Full examples for upload,
  download, list, delete, multipart, presigned URLs, and error handling
- [JavaScript (AWS SDK v3)](https://docs.fil.one/sdk/javascript): Full examples
  for Node.js including streaming downloads, pagination, presigned URLs, and
  error handling; requires `forcePathStyle: true`
- [Go (aws-sdk-go-v2)](https://docs.fil.one/sdk/go): Full examples for upload,
  download, paginated listing, presigned URLs, and error handling; requires
  `UsePathStyle: true`

## Optional

- [Using the Dashboard](https://docs.fil.one/app/overview): Browser-based UI at
  app.fil.one for bucket creation, file upload/download, API key management, and
  billing — no code required
- [FAQ](https://docs.fil.one/faq): Common questions on S3 compatibility,
  blockchain/crypto requirements, data residency, deletion behavior, migration
  from AWS S3, backups, durability, and Filecoin data integrity mechanics
- [Limits & Quotas](https://docs.fil.one/limits): Account limits (storage,
  egress, buckets), object size limits (max 5 TB, use multipart above 5 GB),
  API rate limits, and bucket/key naming constraints
